post_text: If you are new here, this may help orient you.  Tides of Knowing is a long-form editorial and educational platform. It explores tarot as symbolic language, reflective practice and trained perception.  It is not entertainment tarot. It is not prediction content. It is not generic spirituality.  The site brings together articles, field notes, interpretive tools and teaching frameworks designed to strengthen symbolic literacy over time.  Leigh Spencer built this work across decades of journalism, business, mentoring and card reading.  Read the About page: https://www.tidesofknowing.com/about/

post_text: Leigh Spencer brings two worlds together at Tides of Knowing.  Thirty years of journalism and editorial work taught her precision, context and responsibility with language.  Decades of card reading taught her that symbols move before stories settle.  The platform exists so readers can practice tarot with both: structure and intuition, symbolic literacy and reflective discipline.  If that combination interests you, meet the work here: https://www.tidesofknowing.com/about/

post_text: After you meet Leigh Spencer and Tides of Knowing, you may notice one teaching framework: the COMPASS Method.  Center, Open, Map, Perceive, Align, Sense, Seal. A structured path through symbolic reading, not a shortcut to answers.  It supports reflective practice, not prediction.  About page: https://www.tidesofknowing.com/about/  #COMPASSMethod #TidesOfKnowing

post_text: A note for new readers.  Leigh Spencer did not arrive at Tides of Knowing through a single moment. Journalism, editorial leadership, business building and decades of card reading converged into one question: what if tarot were taught as symbolic literacy instead of fortune telling?  That question became a platform. Articles, field notes, interpretive tools and teaching frameworks now live under one editorial roof.  If you want the fuller origin story and what the work is not, the About page is the clearest entry: https://www.tidesofknowing.com/about/
